text.title: two-pass-reason-then-extract Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for two-pass-reason-then-extract. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: When a task requires deep reasoning AND a strict schema, run the strong model in free-text or extended-thinking mode for the reasoning step, then run a cheap extractor model with strict structured output to fill the schema from the strong model's transcript.
rule.rationale: The grammar mask of strict JSON costs probability mass on schema-conforming tokens that are not the optimal reasoning tokens. The SLOT paper and the BuildMVPFast 2026 guide both report 10–15 percent accuracy loss on math and complex analysis when reasoning is forced through strict SO versus free text. Two-pass restores accuracy because the reasoning step has no schema constraint, and the extraction step is easy enough that a small model meets the spec at fraction of the cost.

rule.statement: Use two-pass when (a) the task is reasoning-heavy AND (b) extraction-only accuracy on the cheap model exceeds 95 percent on a held-out sample. If extraction quality is below that threshold, either thicken the schema descriptions or fall back to single-pass on the strong model with an embedded scratchpad.
rule.rationale: Reasoning improvement on the strong model dominates the bill. The extractor is cheap enough that its only constraint is correctness; if a Haiku-class model meets 95+ percent on extraction-only inputs, the savings from preserving the strong model's accuracy justify the second call.

rule.statement: When skip two-pass: latency budget under one second total (two calls cannot fit); schema is simple enough that strict mode accuracy gap is negligible; extractor accuracy on extraction-only is below 95 percent; task volume is so high that the extra call cost dominates.
rule.rationale: Two-pass trades wall-clock time for accuracy and cost efficiency. Measure end-to-end before optimizing; verify extractor quality on held-out extraction-only samples.
